title: Hi, I’m Justin Tan 👋
text:
I’m a student at the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT), passionate about data analytics, machine learning, and building useful digital tools.
I enjoy exploring datasets, uncovering patterns, and turning raw numbers into meaningful insights. Outside of academics, I like hiking, photography, and experimenting with new recipes.

title: About
text:
For my Final Year Project (FYP), I built a Sentiment Analysis Dashboard that analyses customer reviews for e-commerce platforms.


The project aimed to help businesses quickly understand customer sentiment from large volumes of product reviews, without manually reading each one.
Key features:

Scraped and cleaned product review data from public datasets
Applied NLP techniques to classify sentiment (positive, neutral, negative)
Built an interactive dashboard using R Shiny to visualise trends over time







Tool
Purpose




R / tidyverse
Data cleaning and analysis


tidytext
Text processing


R Shiny
Dashboard development


ggplot2
Data visualisation

title: About
section: Reflection on the Use of GenAI
text:
Reflection on the Use of GenAI
For this lab, I used Claude (by Anthropic) to help me build and polish this website.

How it helped

Formatting assistance — helped me structure content in Markdown quickly
Debugging Quarto — spotted an indentation error in my YAML header
Content suggestions — helped me brainstorm and frame my FYP clearly



What I found challenging

GenAI occasionally gave confident but slightly incorrect Quarto-specific syntax — I had to verify against official documentation
It works best when you already understand the tools well enough to evaluate its suggestions



Overall Reflection
Using GenAI as a thinking partner rather than a shortcut worked best for me. It accelerates work most effectively when you bring your own understanding to the table.
